name: Ivanovo Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the revolutionary tradition of Ivanovo, the 'first proletarian capital of Russia', and the ardent labour of its weavers
the rivers that powered the textile mills and the sky over the oblast
the gold of the central emblem
purity, and the cloth woven in the oblast

description: A vertical bicolour, red at the hoist and light blue at the fly, charged in the centre with the stylised emblem of the oblast (a young weaver holding a shuttle and a torch).
history: Adopted on 27 May 1998 by the Legislative Assembly of Ivanovo Oblast. The colour split is a deliberate reference to the historic Ivanovo-Voznesensk banner used by the textile workers' soviet of 1905.

features.symbolism: red recalls the revolutionary heritage of Ivanovo (the 1905 textile workers' soviet, one of the first in Russia); light-blue represents the rivers that drove the textile mills and the sky.
a stylised emblem of a young weaver with a shuttle and a torch, drawn from the oblast's coat of arms; references Ivanovo's reputation as the textile capital of Russia and 'city of brides'.
